name: Hot and Sweet Nut Brittle
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
2. Place almonds in a 15 1/2 x 10 1/2-inch jelly-roll pan. Bake for 10 to 15 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally, until golden brown.
3. Cool almonds in the pan on a wire rack.
4. In a heavy 3-quart saucepan, heat vinegar and 2 cups sugar over medium heat until boiling.
5. Continue cooking over medium heat until the mixture turns dark amber in color, stirring occasionally (about 15 to 20 minutes). If using a thermometer, the temperature should read about 360 degrees F.
6. Meanwhile, in a small bowl, mix salt, cumin, coriander, ground red pepper, and the remaining 1 tablespoon sugar.
7. Lightly grease a large cookie sheet.
8. Remove the saucepan from heat. Stir the spice mixture into the hot sugar syrup.
9. Add almonds, stirring until evenly coated.
10. Immediately pour the mixture onto the prepared cookie sheet.
11. Using 2 forks, spread the almond mixture to form a single layer.
12. Cool brittle completely on the cookie sheet on a wire rack.
13. With hands, break brittle into small pieces.
14. Store in a tightly covered jar or tin for up to one month.
